title: Co-Design, Equity, and Accessible Participation · Product Reasoning Brief: Feedback Accountability
summary: The team invites affected people only after scope is fixed and treats feedback as approval rather than shared problem framing and decision power. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp2 feedback accountability increment in co-design plan and equity impact record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw.
Facilitate disagreement with explicit decision rules, psychological safety, evidence capture, dissent preservation, and escalation paths.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2

decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Predict Feedback Accountability · Theory Feedback Accountability
steps.instruction:
theory case — Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Feedback Accountability contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Feedback Accountability · Theory Feedback Accountability.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw. For Predict Feedback Accountability · Theory Feedback Accountability, keep Feedback Accountability connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

Collecting feedback completes the obligation to participants. That claim fails the predict feedback accountability evidence boundary for Feedback Accountability.

theory case — Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw. For Bound Feedback Accountability · Theory Feedback Accountability, keep Feedback Accountability connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Feedback Accountability while producing co-design plan and equity impact record.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. The worked example variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw. A defensible solution applies Feedback Accountability, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to co-design plan and equity impact record.

content.code:
# Worked focus: pp2-feedback-accountability — Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-4
focus: pp2-feedback-accountability
decision-evidence: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Feedback Accountability, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Feedback Accountability record says co-design plan and equity impact record looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect feedback accountability evidence boundary for Feedback Accountability.

For inspect feedback accountability evidence, apply Feedback Accountability without its stated preconditions, omit the co-design plan and equity impact record trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Feedback Accountability for co-design plan and equity impact record.

Keep the Feedback Accountability artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the feedback accountability defect evidence boundary for Feedback Accountability.

steps.title: Defend the Feedback Accountability model · Theory Feedback Accountability
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Feedback Accountability: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. The theory variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw.

Evidence target: Defend the Feedback Accountability model · Theory Feedback Accountability.
steps.why: Retrieving Feedback Accountability through the concrete co-design plan and equity impact record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the Feedback Accountability model · Theory Feedback Accountability, keep Feedback Accountability connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw.
